summary: Add a --store global flag (programada/fresh/unica/pet) that sets x-store-id/x-cluster-id per request, and make the response cache key store-aware.
reason: The API selects the storefront from the x-store-id/x-cluster-id request headers, not from POST /features/stores/select (a no-op for reads). The generator hardcoded x-store-id:1, so every read (cart, delivery, orders) was locked to Programada and Fresh was unreachable. The cache key omitted the store headers, so two stores collided and returned each other's bodies.
validated_outcome: `cart summary --store programada` => R$2.163,79/124 items; `--store fresh` => R$660,23/44 items (was identical before). TestCacheKeyStoreAware guards the cache collision.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Multi-store sniffed APIs should expose a first-class store/tenant selector flag instead of requiring a hand-edited required-headers file with a hardcoded default.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: The store is a per-request header dimension the generator currently flattens to a single constant; it also belongs in the response cache key.
